questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: What does CI primarily automate in a modern delivery pipeline? Which answer is best?
options: Writing product requirements documents
Only production DNS cutovers at midnight
Manual USB deploys to each server
Building and testing every change to catch breakage early
questions.explanation: Continuous Integration builds/tests on each change for fast feedback.

questions.stem: CD (continuous delivery vs deployment) — interview-accurate nuance?
options: They are identical legal terms with no difference
Deployment forbids automation
Delivery means only compiling locally without tests
Delivery keeps main always releasable; deployment may auto-release to prod
questions.explanation: Delivery = releasable artifact; continuous deployment automates the prod release step.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Infrastructure as Code — core benefit? Which answer is best?
options: Versioned, reviewable, repeatable infra instead of click-ops snowflakes
Replacing application source control
Elimination of all cloud bills
Faster mouse clicks in the cloud console exclusively
questions.explanation: IaC makes infra declarative, reviewable, and reproducible.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Mutable vs immutable infrastructure — preferred DevOps stance? Which answer is best?
options: Prefer replace-in-place golden images/containers over SSH snowflake edits
Immutability means never deploying again
Mutable hosts are required for Kubernetes
Always SSH and apt-get upgrade prod hosts by hand forever
questions.explanation: Immutable deploys replace instances/images rather than long-lived hand edits.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Blue-green deployment — what risk does it mainly reduce? Which answer is best?
options: Database schema design
Long mixed-version rollouts by switching traffic between two full environments
DNS entirely
The need for any automated tests
questions.explanation: Blue-green swaps traffic between parallel stacks for fast rollback.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Canary releases — key idea? Which answer is best?
options: Ship to a small traffic slice first, watch metrics, then promote
All users get the new build simultaneously always
Deploy only on Fridays with no metrics
Canaries replace unit tests
questions.explanation: Canaries limit blast radius while validating real production behavior.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Rolling updates — characteristic trade-off? Which answer is best?
options: Gradual instance replacement with mixed versions during the rollout window
Instant atomic cutover with zero mixed versions ever
No need for health checks
Only works offline
questions.explanation: Rolling updates are incremental; design for compatibility during overlap.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Feature flags — DevOps/product value? Which answer is best?
options: Replace source control branching
Encrypt disks automatically
Remove the need for monitoring
Decouple deploy from release; control exposure and kill switches
questions.explanation: Flags let you deploy dark and release/rollback features independently.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: What should a pipeline gate on before prod promote? Which answer is best?
options: Nothing — push straight from laptop
Manual FTP of node_modules
Only the CEO's Slack emoji
Automated tests, security scans, and successful staging/canary signals as policy requires
questions.explanation: Quality and security gates protect production promotions.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Artifacts and immutability — good practice? Which answer is best?
options: Skip versioning tags
Build once, promote the same artifact across environments
Commit secrets into the artifact for convenience
Rebuild differently per env with unchecked drift
questions.explanation: Same artifact through envs avoids 'works in staging' rebuild drift.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Docker image layers — why do they matter operationally? Which answer is best?
options: Layers eliminate the need for base OS security patches
One layer means no filesystem
Layers replace orchestrators
Layer caching speeds builds and sharing; order Dockerfile for cache hits
questions.explanation: Layered images cache efficiently when Dockerfiles are ordered well.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Multi-stage Docker builds — primary win? Which answer is best?
options: Disabling HEALTHCHECK forever
Mandatory root in production containers
Smaller final images by leaving build toolchains in earlier stages
Slower builds with larger attack surface always
questions.explanation: Multi-stage keeps compile tools out of the runtime image.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Kubernetes Pod vs Deployment — crisp distinction? Which answer is best?
options: Deployment is a single container; Pod manages clusters
Pods replace Services for load balancing
Pod is the runnable unit; Deployment manages replica sets and rolling updates
They are synonyms in the API
questions.explanation: Pods run containers; Deployments control desired replica lifecycle.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: K8s Service — main job? Which answer is best?
options: Stable virtual IP/DNS to reach a dynamic set of Pod endpoints
Building container images
Replacing Ingress TLS everywhere without config
Storing persistent secrets only
questions.explanation: Services provide stable networking to changing Pod IPs.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: ConfigMaps vs Secrets — operational difference? Which answer is best?
options: ConfigMaps cannot mount as files
Both inject config; Secrets are for sensitive data with extra access controls (still encode carefully)
Secrets are plain env files committed to git publicly
ConfigMaps encrypt with HSM by default always
questions.explanation: Secrets are the sensitive channel; still protect etcd/RBAC and prefer external secret stores.

questions.stem: Liveness vs readiness probes — interview distinction?
options: Readiness kills nodes; liveness only affects DNS TTL
They are identical HTTP checks with same effect
Liveness restarts unhealthy containers; readiness controls traffic until ready
Neither should be used in production
questions.explanation: Liveness = restart; readiness = in/out of Service endpoints.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler — scales based on what typically? Which answer is best?
options: Git commit message length
Number of Dockerfiles in the repo
Metrics like CPU/custom metrics against targets to change replica count
Manual SSH only
questions.explanation: HPA adjusts replicas from resource/custom metrics.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Requests vs limits in K8s — why set both thoughtfully? Which answer is best?
options: Both must be zero for Guaranteed QoS
Limits are ignored; only requests matter for memory
Requests throttle CPU; limits schedule pods
Requests drive scheduling/guarantee; limits cap usage and affect throttling/OOM behavior
questions.explanation: Requests place pods; limits bound consumption — mis-sets cause noise/OOM.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Helm — what problem does it solve? Which answer is best?
options: Packaging and templating Kubernetes manifests as versioned charts
Terminating TLS at the browser
Replacing container runtimes
Building Linux kernels
questions.explanation: Helm packages deployable K8s applications with values/templates.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: GitOps — core operating model? Which answer is best?
options: SSH into nodes and edit YAML live with no history
Desired cluster state lives in Git; a controller reconciles continuously
Only apply manifests from Slack bots without review
Forbid pull requests for infra
questions.explanation: Git is the source of truth; operators sync clusters to it.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Prometheus — primary role in the stack? Which answer is best?
options: Log aggregation replacement for all syslog
Distributed tracing UI only
CI artifact registry
Pull-based metrics collection and time-series storage for alerting/query
questions.explanation: Prometheus scrapes metrics and evaluates alert rules.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Grafana — typical pairing? Which answer is best?
options: Building container images
Replacing etcd
Dashboards and visualization over metrics/logs/traces datasources
Issuing TLS certs only
questions.explanation: Grafana visualizes observability data sources.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Four golden signals (Google SRE) — which set? Which answer is best?
options: CPU only, forever
Disk brand, rack color, PSU wattage, fan RPM only
Latency, traffic, errors, saturation
Commits, PRs, tickets, emojis
questions.explanation: Golden signals: latency, traffic, errors, saturation.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: RED metrics for request-driven services? Which answer is best?
options: RPC, ETL, DDL
Rate, errors, duration
RAM, EEPROM, DVD
RAID, ECC, DMA
questions.explanation: RED = rate, errors, duration for services.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: USE method for resources? Which answer is best?
options: Unit, suite, e2e only
Utilization, saturation, errors
UUID, SHA, ECDSA
Upload, stream, encode
questions.explanation: USE examines utilization, saturation, and errors of resources.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Structured logging — why prefer it? Which answer is best?
options: Disabling timestamps
Logging secrets in plaintext for debug
Longer free-text novels without fields
Machine-parseable fields enable reliable querying and correlation
questions.explanation: Structured logs (JSON etc.) power search and analytics.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Correlation/trace IDs across microservices — purpose? Which answer is best?
options: Encrypt passwords
Shard databases
Replace authentication
Stitch a request's path across services/logs/traces
questions.explanation: IDs correlate distributed request journeys.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: OpenTelemetry — high-level pitch? Which answer is best?
options: A Kubernetes distro
A CI SaaS only
A packet sniffer replacing Wireshark exclusively
Vendor-neutral instrumentation APIs for traces/metrics/logs export
questions.explanation: OTel standardizes telemetry instrumentation and export.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: SLI vs SLO vs SLA — correct ordering of meaning? Which answer is best?
options: SLI measures; SLO targets; SLA is the business/contract consequence
SLO is always 100% with no error budget
SLA is a Prometheus metric type; SLI is a legal contract
They are interchangeable acronyms
questions.explanation: Measure (SLI), target (SLO), agreement (SLA).

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Error budgets — how do they guide releases? Which answer is best?
options: They only apply to frontend CSS
Remaining reliability budget informs how aggressively you can ship vs freeze
They replace on-call rotations
They ban all deploys forever
questions.explanation: Error budgets balance velocity against reliability spend.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: On-call alert hygiene — best practice? Which answer is best?
options: No runbooks; tribal knowledge only
Alert on symptoms/user impact with runbooks; avoid noisy low-value pages
Disable paging on weekends always
Page on every DEBUG log line
questions.explanation: Actionable, symptom-based alerts with runbooks reduce toil/fatigue.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Incident severity — why classify? Which answer is best?
options: Aligns response urgency, comms, and postmortem depth to impact
To avoid timelines in write-ups
Only for billing marketing
So you never need a commander
questions.explanation: Sev levels drive process intensity and stakeholder communication.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Blameless postmortems — intent? Which answer is best?
options: Learn from systemic causes without punishing individuals for honest mistakes
Hide timelines from the team
Find a scapegoat quickly
Skip action items
questions.explanation: Blameless culture improves learning and future prevention.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Secrets management — preferred pattern? Which answer is best?
options: Email passwords in Slack forever
Bake long-lived root keys into all images
Short-lived credentials from a vault/cloud secret manager; never commit secrets
Commit .env with prod passwords to public Git
questions.explanation: Central secret stores + rotation + least privilege beat repo secrets.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Supply chain security — SBOM relevance? Which answer is best?
options: They disable CI
SBOMs are only for hardware BOM shopping
Inventory of dependencies helps vulnerability response and provenance
SBOMs replace code review
questions.explanation: SBOMs document components for vuln and compliance workflows.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Image scanning in CI — what does it catch? Which answer is best?
options: Logic bugs in business rules always
Keyboard layout issues
Known CVEs and misconfigurations in container dependencies/base images
DNS misconfiguration exclusively
questions.explanation: Scanners flag vulnerable packages and common image issues.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Least privilege IAM — DevOps angle? Which answer is best?
options: Everyone gets account-admin for speed
Grant minimal permissions needed; prefer roles over long-lived admin keys
Share one access key in the wiki
Disable MFA for CI bots always
questions.explanation: Least privilege and short-lived creds reduce blast radius.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Network policies / security groups — purpose? Which answer is best?
options: Increase MTU only
Compile Go binaries
Replace TLS
Restrict which workloads can talk on which ports/paths
questions.explanation: Network controls enforce allowlists between components.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Terraform state — why is it sensitive/important? Which answer is best?
options: State is unused if you use modules
It should be committed with secrets freely
It maps real resources; corruption/leaks can break infra or expose data
It is only a README
questions.explanation: Remote locked state is critical; protect and backup it.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Drift detection — what problem does it address? Which answer is best?
options: JVM garbage collection
Manual console changes diverging from IaC desired state
CSS browser quirks
Git merge conflicts in application code only
questions.explanation: Drift tools/reconcile when reality leaves the declared config.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Chaos engineering — responsible practice? Which answer is best?
options: Replace backups with chaos
Run controlled experiments to find weaknesses, with blast radius limits and abort criteria
Only on Fridays before holidays
Randomly delete prod with no hypothesis or rollback
questions.explanation: Chaos is scientific, scoped, and reversible — not reckless sabotage.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Disaster recovery RPO vs RTO? Which answer is best?
options: RTO only applies to CI runners
They are the same metric
RPO is uptime percent; RTO is packet loss
RPO = max data loss tolerance; RTO = max time to restore service
questions.explanation: RPO is data-loss window; RTO is recovery time target.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Backup 3-2-1 rule — meaning? Which answer is best?
options: 3 alerts, 2 dashboards, 1 silence
3 regions, 2 VPCs, 1 subnet only
3 copies, 2 media types, 1 offsite/offline
3 commits, 2 branches, 1 tag
questions.explanation: Classic backup diversity: copies, media, offsite.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Database migration in zero-downtime deploys — safe pattern? Which answer is best?
options: Expand/contract (compatible multi-step) migrations with backward-compatible app versions
Rewrite all data in a single locking migration during peak
Ignore migrations and edit prod by hand
Drop columns first while old app still reads them
questions.explanation: Expand/contract keeps old and new app versions compatible during rollout.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Sidecar pattern — common use? Which answer is best?
options: Helper container for proxy/logging/secrets beside the app container
A Helm repository mirror
A type of PV storage class only
Replacing the main business container always
questions.explanation: Sidecars add cross-cutting capabilities next to the app.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Service mesh (e.g. Istio/Linkerd) — value prop? Which answer is best?
options: Replacing CI
mTLS, traffic policy, and observability for east-west service traffic
Building mobile apps
Storing Terraform state
questions.explanation: Meshes handle secure service-to-service networking and telemetry.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: Admission controllers in Kubernetes — role? Which answer is best?
options: Scrape Prometheus metrics
Build Dockerfiles
Schedule pods onto nodes exclusively
Intercept API requests to validate/mutate objects before persistence
questions.explanation: Admission webhooks/policies gate what enters the cluster API.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: PodDisruptionBudget — why set one? Which answer is best?
options: Increase CPU limits automatically
Store secrets
Disable rolling updates
Limit voluntary disruptions so enough replicas stay available during drains
questions.explanation: PDBs protect availability during voluntary node/pod disruptions.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: ephemeral vs persistent volumes — choose when? Which answer is best?
options: Persistent volumes replace object storage CDNs
Always ephemeral for databases in prod
Ephemeral for scratch; PVC/PV for data that must survive pod restarts
PVCs cannot attach to pods
questions.explanation: Match volume lifetime to data durability needs.

questions.stem: In an interview, you are asked: FinOps / cloud cost hygiene — DevOps-relevant habit? Which answer is best?
options: Ignore bills until quarterly surprise
Leave unused GPUs on 24/7 with no tags
Disable autoscaling to maximize spend
Tag resources, right-size, turn off idle, watch unit cost with usage
questions.explanation: Cost visibility and rightsizing are part of operational excellence.
